data.title: BTCS, a US-listed company, will raise $57.8 million to hoard ETH
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Blockchain technology company BTCS (NASDAQ: BTCS) announced on Wednesday that it has reached an agreement with ATW Partners to raise up to $57.8 million through convertible bonds, all of which will be used to acquire Ethereum (ETH). The first $7.8 million bond has been issued, and the remaining $50 million will be launched in batches according to the negotiation between the two parties.
BTCS Chairman and CEO Charles Allen said: "We are following MicroStrategy's strategy of accumulating Bitcoin and systematically increasing Ethereum holdings."
It is reported that BTCS mainly operates Ethereum/BNBChain verification nodes, crypto asset staking and on-chain data analysis business.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs had a total net inflow of $320 million yesterday, and none of the twelve ETFs had a net outflow
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (May 14, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was US$320 million.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Blackrock ETF IBIT, with a single-day net inflow of US$233 million. Currently, IBIT's total net inflow has reached US$45.013 billion.
The second is Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a single-day net inflow of US$36.1349 million. Currently, FBTC's total net inflow has reached US$11.651 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is US$121.845 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Bitcoin's total market value) is 5.92%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$41.399 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETF had a total net inflow of $63.4699 million yesterday, and none of the nine ETFs had a net outflow
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (May 14, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Ethereum spot ETFs was $63.4699 million.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was Blackrock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $57.6085 million per day. Currently, ETHA's total net inflow has reached $4.255 billion.
The second is Fidelity ETF FETH, with a net inflow of $5.8614 million per day. Currently, FETH's total net inflow has reached $1.425 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $9.04 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Ethereum's total market value) is 2.88%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $2.529 billion.

data.title: Japan’s carmakers are expecting $19 billion in losses from US tariffs
data.description:

The industry’s leading brands say the duties will squeeze financial performance this year and beyond. Some firms have even stopped giving guidance while they […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/japans-carmakers-19-billion-loss-tariffs/

data.title: Hong Kong Enhances Security Measures for Virtual Asset Ecosystem
data.description: According to PANews, the Hong Kong Police Force's Cyber Security and Technology Crime Bureau has released an update on the security maintenance of the city's virtual asset ecosystem. Chief Superintendent Lam Cheuk-ho emphasized the shared responsibility among exchanges, regulatory bodies, and law enforcement agencies in ensuring security. The bureau has collaborated with the University of Hong Kong to develop CryptoTrace, a virtual asset analysis tool designed to provide advanced intelligence and investigative support for crime investigation units. Additionally, a new anti-fraud team has been established in the New Territories South region.

data.title: Polygon Co-Founder Warns of AI-Driven Scams Targeting Crypto Startups
data.description:
According to PANews, Polygon's co-founder Sandeep Nailwal has recently revealed that scammers are using AI technology to create fake video representations of him to deceive crypto industry startups. The fraudsters initially hacked the Telegram account of Polygon Ventures head Shreyansh Singh and then contacted startups within the ecosystem under the guise of investment discussions, luring victims into Zoom meetings. During these meetings, the scammers used AI-generated images of Nailwal, Singh, and fictitious team members without audio interaction, and tricked participants into installing malicious software development kits.

Nailwal noted that several entrepreneurs have approached him to verify the authenticity of these meetings. He advised community members to refrain from installing unfamiliar software during unsolicited interactions and emphasized the importance of operational protocols. He urged crypto users to separate wallet signing activities from everyday device usage, stating, "These attack methods are becoming increasingly sophisticated, so the best practice is to use a dedicated laptop solely for wallet signing operations and absolutely not use it for anything unrelated to wallets."

data.title: Bitcoin Retail Demand Rises 3.4% As Small Investors Return To The Market – New ATH Soon?
data.description:
Retail participation in the Bitcoin (BTC) market is on the rise, as on-chain data indicates that smaller investors are gradually re-entering the space. This renewed activity is often a sign of growing confidence in the asset and can act as a catalyst for the next leg up in price. Bitcoin Witnesses Rise In Retail Participation According to a recent CryptoQuant Quicktake post by on-chain analyst Carmelo Aleman, retail investors – defined as wallets holding less than $10,000 worth of BTC – are steadily returning to the market. These participants are typically the most reactive to market movements
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-retail-demand-rises-3-4-as-small-investors-return-to-the-market-new-ath-soon/

data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, LBank has launched Axelrod by Virtuals (AXR) at 11:00 (SGT) on May 15, and opened the AXR/USDT trading pair. Currently, the deposit service is open, and the withdrawal service will be open at 11:00 on May 16.

data.title: Ukraine Eyes Bitcoin for National Reserves in First-for-Europe Move
data.description:
In a move that feels both futuristic and kinda inevitable, Ukraine is working on a law that would let it officially hold Bitcoin as part of its national reserves. If they pull this off, they’ll be one of the first countries in Europe to go beyond crypto-friendly buzzwords and actually put digital assets to work.
source: https://99bitcoins.com/news/bitcoin-btc/ukraine-eyes-bitcoin-for-national-reserves-in-first-for-europe-move/

data.title: The total subscription amount of Gate.io Launchpad's first project Puffverse (PFVS) exceeded 41 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the subscription of Gate.io Launchpad's first project Puffverse (PFVS) is in progress, and the total investment amount has exceeded 41 million USDT in 48 hours. In order to enhance the flexibility of participation and optimize the user experience, Gate.io has simultaneously launched the "repurchase" function, allowing users to replenish funds unlimited times during the project subscription period.
According to DefiLlama data, driven by this Launchpad subscription activity, Gate.io's net inflow in the past 24 hours ranked second among global trading platforms.

data.title: The crypto market generally fell back, with the Layer2 sector leading the decline by 7.02%, and ETH falling below $2,600
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News On May 15, according to SoSoValue data, the crypto market sectors generally pulled back. The Layer2 and GameFi sectors, which rose sharply yesterday, fell by 7.02% and 6.99% respectively. In the Layer2 sector, Starknet (STRK) fell by 9.64% in 24 hours, and Celestia (TIA) fell by 9.72%. In the GameFi sector, Axie Infinity (AXS) and Decentraland (MANA) fell by 9.05% and 10.65% respectively.
In addition, Ethereum (ETH) broke through $2,700 yesterday and began to fall back, falling 3.52% in 24 hours and falling below $2,600. Bitcoin (BTC) fell by 0.68%, but still remained around $103,000.
In other sectors, the PayFi sector fell 1.85%, and the CeFi sector fell 1.99%. Among them, LEO Token (LEO) and Amp (AMP) rose 2.00% and 13.75% against the trend respectively; the Layer1 sector fell 4.53%, and TRON (TRX) rose slightly by 0.55%; the Meme sector fell 4.60%, the DeFi sector fell 4.86%, and Ethena (ENA) fell 8.85%.
The crypto sector index reflecting the historical market conditions of the sector shows that the ssiLayer2, ssiGameFi, and ssiSocialFi indexes fell 7.89%, 7.11%, and 6.02% respectively in 24 hours.
